prompt: The sorted set does not sort correctly. What is the bug?
choices: Item does not implement Comparable<Item>; TreeSet requires elements to be Comparable or a Comparator must be provided at construction
TreeSet cannot have more than one element
int fields are not sortable in a TreeSet
explanation: TreeSet keeps elements sorted. To sort, it must compare them. Item implements no Comparable interface. Calling add() throws ClassCastException at runtime. Fix: implement Comparable<Item> in Item (return Integer.compare(this.value, other.value)), or provide a Comparator: new TreeSet<>(Comparator.comparingInt(i -> i.value)).
